  "weights": {

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To make this stronger, which path should we prioritize: A) prose and clarity polish without altering claims (R1.6), B) strengthening the argument by tightening the contribution framing, logic, and claim–evidence alignment (R1.1–R1.3; may adjust claim wording), or C) higher-level restructuring and positioning vs prior work, including novelty framing and citations (R1.4–R1.5, R1.7)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening may require sharpening the thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level.",
    "R1.2": "A stronger piece often hinges on a coherent, internally consistent argument from premise to conclusion.",
    "R1.3": "Ensuring that stated claims match the reported results is central to making the work convincingly stronger.",
    "R1.6": "Improved clarity, concision, and tone can substantially increase the perceived strength of the writing.",
    "RX.1": "Explicitly flagging limits and uncertainties avoids overstating the strength of the results.",
    "RX.2": "Guarding against overclaiming ensures improvements do not exceed what the evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work must stay within the scope of ‘make this stronger’ without adding new experiments or content not in the artifact.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding misleading emphases reduces downstream risks such as misapplication or misinterpretation.",
    "RX.5": "Any strengthening should be traceable to the provided text and clearly attributable to its sources."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To make this stronger, which path should we prioritize: A) prose and clarity polish without altering claims (R1.6), B) strengthening the argument by tightening the contribution framing, logic, and claim–evidence alignment (R1.1–R1.3; may adjust claim wording), or C) higher-level restructuring and positioning vs prior work, including novelty framing and citations (R1.4–R1.5, R1.7)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening may require sharpening the thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: A stronger piece often hinges on a coherent, internally consistent argument from premise to conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: Ensuring that stated claims match the reported results is central to making the work convincingly stronger.
rationale.R1.6: Improved clarity, concision, and tone can substantially increase the perceived strength of the writing.
rationale.RX.1: Explicitly flagging limits and uncertainties avoids overstating the strength of the results.
rationale.RX.2: Guarding against overclaiming ensures improvements do not exceed what the evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work must stay within the scope of ‘make this stronger’ without adding new experiments or content not in the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding misleading emphases reduces downstream risks such as misapplication or misinterpretation.
rationale.RX.5: Any strengthening should be traceable to the provided text and clearly attributable to its sources.
